title: Oxydectes zeylanica
taxonomy: Oxydectes zeylanica is classified under the family Arecaceae, commonly known as the palm family, due to its palmate leaf structure and growth habit. This classification places it within the order Arecales, a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants primarily recognized by their palmate foliage. As a member of the family Arecaceae, Oxydectes zeylanica shares characteristics with other palm species, including a single stem and pinnate or palmate leaves. The genus Oxydectes is further classified within the subfamily Coryphoideae, a subgroup of palms that exhibit a distinctive palmate leaf arrangement. By classifying Oxydectes zeylanica within the Arecaceae family, botanists are able to group it with other plants that share similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. intro: Oxydectes zeylanica, also known as Indian sarsaparilla or Oxydendrum, is a plant species native to Sri Lanka, belonging to the family Ericaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxydectes zeylanica include saponins, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis and eczema.
